All right, welcome back. So we're not done scaling. All right, we've had some pretty good luck so far. So if you remember, there are two ways that we can scale. All right, so let's draw a big line down the middle. Number one, we have vertical and number two, we have horizontal. And vertical is where you just take an audience. All right, so we can say audience number one. And what we do is we just increase the budget here every day or so until this stops working. Eventually it will stop working, by the way, but it kind of makes sense, you know, you just take an audience that's working and you just spend more money on that audience. And if all things remain constant, you're good. Eventually things do break, which is totally normal. And we talked about at the very beginning of our time together how to fix that. So we won't go into that now. In terms of horizontal scaling, what we typically do here is instead of taking like this one audience and just spending more money on it, we take an audience or a campaign and we keep reusing it in different ways. All right, so we could have, let's do it and what I'm going to show you how to do right now is we'll do it at the campaign level. So we can take the original campaign. All right, so we've got that. And so this one's running now, you know, and then so that's these guys. Okay, these fellas here. All right, so we can take that original campaign we've got them. Then we can take new versions of these campaigns and do different things with them. So this can be OC original campaign two, and we can have like one bidding strategy here. And then we can take this one OC three, and we can duplicate that campaign again, and we can use a different ad. And that's what we're going to do now. So what we're going to focus on in this particular video is we're going to do this one OC two, we're not actually going to call it OC two, it just kind of sounds cool, you know, like sciencey. But there's a fancy way saying just copying this campaign and running in a different way. So let's have a look at our results, by the way, I'm filming this for you like 24 hours or so after I shot the last video. Here are the ones that we messed with. Okay, and as you can see, you know, we got rid of the ad sets that weren't working and we kept to we raised the budgets to $10 a day for each of these. So it's nothing crazy. Yeah. So this was only spending $20 a day, it'll take forever at this rate until we actually see anything happen. But look like this one has actually gotten to sales. So this is still a producing customers incredibly well. So for whatever reason, you know, we're getting really cheap customers from the United Kingdom, which is great. I'm thrilled to get it. Not a lot has happened from our friends in Canada. That's how you say that. Yeah, that's okay, whatever, you know, I mean, we've spent $3 so far for today on this particular campaign in that audience. And again, I'm looking at high-risk data, but it's the same thing here. The reason I keep using high-risk data is because it takes a little while for Facebook to catch up, you know, I guess it's not showing those purchases in Facebook stats yet. For the day. And that's okay. You know, I mean, we're not going to lose any sleep over that. We know that Facebook can take their gosh dang time sometimes with this stuff. You know, they don't get in a big hurry. So whatever, you know, we're going to be okay. But we know that like so far, everything's looking pretty good here. And then if we look at this other one, this is the remaining audiences for that campaign that had a whole bunch of audiences. We got rid of all the ones except for the ones that were not getting customers within our price range. And we haven't made any money with this one yet. But it's not really a big deal. We've spent $32, you know, and some days, you know, it'll be terrible and some days will be good. And that's okay. I'm not going to draw any conclusions based on this yet because in each audience, we've barely spent any money. Right. And that's really deliberate. Like in our beginning stages of building new campaigns, we don't want to just be throwing money around. Right. So we want to be very strategic about this, which is why we have our next step. So I'm going to come over here to the Facebook. All right. And the first thing I'm going to do is come back over here to the campaign level. All right. So if we remember this one that we edited yesterday, these are the ones that are targeting like everybody between certain age ranges and different countries. I'm going to take this one and I'm going to come over here and I'm going to get rid of these adsets. They didn't perform. I turned them off, but I'm just going to delete them. All right. So I'm going to do that. And the reason I'm going to do that is because what we're about to do in our next step here, which is this, I'm going to take this campaign right here and I'm going to copy it. All right. So I'm just going to hit duplicate and I'm going to set the new version up a little bit differently. All right. So watch this. I'll hit duplicate here. All right. Create new campaign. Number of copies is one. We don't have to do anything except for hit duplicate. All right. And instead of running this exactly the way we did the first one, what I'm going to do is I'm going to change the way the campaign is run. So I'm going to change this from, it's called ABO, AdSET bid optimization, to CBO, which is campaign budget optimization. And what this means is that essentially Facebook is going to come into this campaign. It's going to go, I'm going to spend the money between these adsets. However, I damn well want to, right, based on which ones I think are going to produce the best results. And sometimes this is amazing and sometimes it sucks. All right. So like the only way we're going to know is to test it. So the first thing I'm going to do is I'm going to come over here. I'm going to edit this campaign here. This is going to take us right back to where we were. And I'm going to call this one CBO. All right. So that's pretty simple. And I'm just going to turn this to on. Now, when we do this, whenever you turn a campaign into CBO, we no longer have the ability to say, hey, only spend this much per ad set. Instead, you tell it, hey, spend this much per day on this campaign as a whole. All right. And what I'm going to do is I'm actually going to raise this budget a little bit. And so the reason I'm doing this is because there's only two audiences in here and I want to get a lot of data really quickly. So generally speaking, if I were doing this and I wasn't teaching it and I was trying to really play by the rules, I might say, okay, you know, let's raise this up to $40 and maybe I would let it run for two days. And if it wasn't working right, I would kill it. Right. Because, you know, I'll be spending 80 bucks across two different audiences and we'll see how they do. But in this case, what I want to do is I'm going to raise this guy up. I'm going to raise it up a little bit more, you know, just to just to get us some more data. Now, what you should know is when you do this, when you go from, you know, an audience that you're used to spending $5 a day and then, you know, you go to where it's potentially spending 10 times more on that, your first day might not be that good. So please don't let it freak you out. It's because Facebook's artificial intelligence is like, all right, we're going to go like go hog wild crazy and see where everybody is and we're going to show this to a whole lot more people. And some of those people might be the wrong people and that's okay. Now, we could just hit publish and be done here. And if we were, Facebook would take this $50 budget and it would just spend it. Whoops. I'll go over here. It would spend it between these two ad sets. However, it saw fit. You know, it'd be like, all right, well, we're getting customers from you. Okay, so let's put all the money in the UK. And that's a good way to do it. However, what I want to do is I want to make sure that each ad set actually gets a chance. All right, so what I'm going to do here is I'm going to say, okay, Facebook, I'm going to let you decide which audience to spend the budget on. But I'm going to set a minimum so you have to at least spend a certain amount on each of these. And the reason why is because like, you never know, you know, sometimes Facebook algorithm, it'll think one is better and it just will completely ignore the other one. And it won't give the other one a chance. Right. So here's how we do that. Okay, I'm just going to do this individually by ad set. I'm like, going to click here on ad set. All right, I'll click edit. I'm going to name this just as CBO. It doesn't matter, but it's for my own OCD weirdness. All right. And so what you do is if you want to make it where it has to spend a certain percentage of that budget every day, first of all, look, almost missed this, gotta un-click that or else it'll mess you up. We got to come down here and it's right around here somewhere. Hang on, attribution setting. Let's see. They've moved it because I'm on camera. There's a little place where we can tell it how much we want to spend each day on this particular ad set here. And of course, pause the camera while I find it. And of course, I found it right after I paused it. It's right here over budget and schedule. So what you want to do is you click show more options. All right. Then you click edit. And then we click on add spend the limits. And I want to say, okay, you know, we're spending a total in this whole campaign. We're spending $50 total between two ad sets here. And I want it to spend a minimum of $10 on each one, just to make sure, all right, that it's giving each one a fair shake. And then it can take that remaining money and allocate it to whichever one it's doing better. All right. And so I have that clicked here. And I'm going to just come over here. All right. And so that's all we got to do. That's it. All right. So I'm going to come over here. I'm going to just go to the next audience and I'm going to do the same thing. So I just click edit here. All right. I'm going to add CBO here. You don't have to. This is literally just for, you know, my own naming conventions. And all we have to do is come over here. And then I'm just going to say, all right, you know, spend $10 minimum. On this one. Okay. So this way we're preventing the algorithm from completely ignoring any particular audience. All right. And so you might be thinking, All right, dude, wait a minute, you know, so first of all, let me go ahead and publish this. So when I hit publish, it'll publish everything we just did. So we didn't change the ads or anything. You know, we just cloned it. Right. And made these changes. But one thing you might be wondering is, okay, dude, wait a minute, you know, you're, I just made that one disappear, but probably because it's in draft here, most likely. And I have that thing set to active. Yeah. So let me get rid of that. There we go. Okay. So you might be thinking, All right, man, look, aren't you competing with yourself now? You know, you have these two campaigns and they're targeting the same audiences. Isn't this going to mess you up? And the answer is no. And the reason why is because the audience sizes, look, these audience sizes, giants, 19 million people. And so one of the cool things about horizontal scaling is, look, we know, even if I said, let's spend a million dollars a day on this audience, it's not going to reach all 19 million people. So whenever you clone a campaign like we just did, it's going to go hunt, start hunting generally speaking in another segment of this audience, which is cool. And it may or may not work. So what we have here, all right, with this campaign, as we're saying, All right, spend 50 bucks a day between these two audiences. And 20 of that 50 bucks, you have to spend 10 of it here and 10 of it here. And then the remaining 30 do whatever you want to do with it. That's what we're telling. All right. And that's, that's it. We're good. I need to publish this one too. Here, let me go ahead and hit that publish that guy. Well, I thought I needed to publish it. Let's see. Turn that sucker on. There we go. And again, the reason you do it is like, their algorithm is pretty smart, but not always. So I know these guys have worked. I know both of these audiences have worked. So essentially what I've done is I've mimicked the other one. All right. I'm going to hit publish here because we're good to go. Okay. And I've duplicated this other one, but I'm giving it, I'm saying like, Hey, look, man, you have to spend $10 a day per audience on this one. But then you have another $70 because, you know, this one is $70 in total every day. This guy right here, you know, has got seven audiences at $10 each. But it's this one is like, you got to spend $10 per audience here. But then you have another $70 left over to spend on whichever one's working best. And, you know, logic would lead us to believe, right, that Facebook's algorithm is going to like take the data that happened from that $10 ad spend and then whichever ad set is working for the day, pour all the money on that one. It sometimes does that, but it doesn't always do that. You know, so it's like, that's why I'm kind of up in the budget something's a little bit to go ahead and show you how it'll get weird in a hurry. So I'm going to let these guys run, you know, and I'm going to let them run for three or four days so we can get enough data here for me to fix stuff that goes wrong because stuff is inevitably going to go wrong. And so this is this is the like the campaign number two of horror of horizontal scale, right? So we've scaled vertically by just increasing the budgets and now we're scaling horizontally by just increasing the number of campaigns that are using those same ad sets. All right. And so our next method of horizontal scale is we've got some ad sets and some audiences that we think are pretty good. Now we hadn't spent a fortune on them, right? So the data is pretty early, but they worked out so far. And now we want to continue to scale by replicating these and adding different ads in. All right. So that's our third phase of this here. And so that is what we will do in the next video. All right. So I'll see you there.
